name: Wizard (Flow Progress)
summary: The reference Flow-Progress surface — a `<wizard-flow>` custom element that wires a portable workflow graph through the swappable `customWorkflowEngine` (#634/#650) into the flow-progress UX (register `wizard`). It owns no orchestration: transitions, guards, and completion stay on the engine; the element renders the current position and each step's status. Composes the Stepper block for the linear-presentation mechanics it already owns (`aria-current="step"`, the Step N of M announcement, one-panel-at-a-time switching) and maps the engine's step-transition stream to per-step status (wait/process/finish/error). Back/undo uses the running instance's `back()` (the movement: back-allowed default). Slice A of #651.

ariaCurrentStep.usage: The active step indicator and its panel carry aria-current="step" (delegated to the composed Stepper); the step list is an ordered <ol> conveying position

multiPageForms.usage: A labelled step region, a visible per-step status indicator, and a polite live region announcing 'Step N of M' on each engine transition
